mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor demand that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized contraction in household balance sheets and durable goods demand, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when housing starts fall but consumer credit remains stable, or the combined proxy combination yields a Rank IC above 0.05 in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight labor markets, rising industrial production reflects capacity expansion that outpaces demand growth, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates positively with equity returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical median acts as a continuous momentum driver for corporate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDP growth beats the median, or the signal generates negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average corporate return on invested capital, forcing equity risk premiums higher and depressing S&P 500 valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand or remain stable despite the funds rate persistently exceeding 4.5%, or the threshold breach fails to produce a statistically significant drop in forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and rising unemployment indicate a turning point in the business cycle, triggering algorithmic risk reduction and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when payroll growth slows but unemployment remains flat, or the interaction term yields a positive Rank IC in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, rapid rebounds in housing starts signal strong consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Housing starts rebound but equity returns remain negative due to supply chain bottlenecks overriding demand signals, or the forward return differential between recovery and non-recovery periods is statistically insignificant.
